title: Deschampsia chrysantha
taxonomy: Deschampsia chrysantha is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Deschampsia, which consists of several species of grasses. Deschampsia chrysantha is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and is often found growing in alpine meadows. The plant is also known as golden-desert hair grass or golden oat grass, due to its golden-colored flowers. Within the family Poaceae, Deschampsia chrysantha is further classified within the subfamily Pooideae.

intro: Deschampsia chrysantha, also known as golden hair grass, is a perennial grass species native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
